title: Accompaniments 1
description: Part of my goal as I started imagining Fruit Music was to release without fear. This was a first exercise toward that. The concept came about from thinking of the term 'pre-condom' referring to the era of gay porn before the AIDS epidemic made condoms essential. With the prevalence and privilege of HIV prevention drugs now, porn has mostly ditched rubbers again, which has kind of defined 'condom' and 'post-condom' eras as well. Always inspired by Patrick Cowley, I wanted to make pseudo soundtracks to these film periods. I made them fast and didn't give myself space to think any more than twice.

title: Sidewalk Snapshots
description: The nice folks at ARYZE commissioned me to create a series extending from my ongoing yyj.jpg project for their annual magazine. I walked more of the city, found more quirks, and wrote about what it feels like to share the space of a city.

title: Devours - XY (Remix)
description: Devours is the best. I wanted to make a gay club track version of this toxic masculinity anthem.

title: yyj.jpg 2024
description: Having lived in Victoria for nearly 20 years, I've seen it change a great deal. Development is a constant here. Entire blocks of the city as it was have faded from my memory and I can only picture what exists now. I love how we describe a place by these changes (it's where ___ used to be) and how we are excited or concerned by them. Initially, I wanted to begin this project as a more fulsome documentation of Victoria's core for comparison over time. The photos would be regularly collected capsule snapshots of the streets and occupants. But there are better archivists around and I had no attraction to the completely neutral imagery it aimed for. Instead, I let the project form itself naturally by simply going out walking with a camera and observing. The dynamic and static parts of the city reveal themselves by just repeatedly being and looking. This first collection is 47 photos taken throughout 2024. They reflect on  how it feels to live here as a place of its own uniqueness and its anywhereness.
credits: Photography, design, and layout by Aaron Bergunder. Printed by Metropol Industries in Victoria. Released through Kinda on Metalabel.

title: Unplugged
description: Hey, why not try to write a whole EP for one show (again)? The Copper Owl wanted me to throw a party during pride in Victoria, so I texted Kye and asked them if they wanted to start a pop duo thing and perform at it. So we wrote this album in a month, sending demos back and forth. Then we wore as little clothing as we felt we could get away with and performed it all at Oof! Really great night. A disastrous second show tarnished our spirits and that was the end. Again, the haste of creating these makes them feel foreign and I love them for that. It was huge to feel like I could write some songs with energy again.
credits: All songs by Aaron Bergunder and Kye Plant. Recorded and mixed by Aaron Bergunder. Cover art by Aaron Bergunder, with Kye's ass in the back cover's glittery starring role.

title: Teen Bitch, Rip Yr Tits Off
description: After years of friendship, Sean Evans and I finally set up in his apartment on Fisgard one hot afternoon and wrote some music. I had these rough sketches of guitar songs and felt like some 2 minute tracks of local jokes would be fun. Those first ones are long lost: Top 5 Burritos, Brown Pants, Dad Safari (a tribute to Bill Stuart’s candid photo series). Then on a trip out to Sled Island Music Festival in 2010, I convinced Kye to join the band and play bass. We mellowed out a bit, but got to play some fun punk shows. No clue where the title came from.
credits: Songs by Aaron Bergunder, Sean Evans, Kye Plant. Recorded by Robert DiNinno and Aaron Bergunder. Mixed by Aaron Bergunder. Cover photo by Kye Plant.

title: Dreamboat - Summerheart
description: This song is about that post-breakup stage of self acceptance and freedom and possibility. So we wrote a pitch to capture that feeling through a blissful, expressive, fleeting summer weekend romance road trip. It was intentionally over-the-top millenial optimism. I think we even called it a pisstake reaction in the deck. Ironically, in our light mockery of cliche summer videos we had a genuinely incredible summer weekend together making it.
credits: Filmed by Aaron Bergunder, Kyle Surovy, and Geoffrey Tomlin-Hood. Edited by Aaron Bergunder. Starring Ian Piears and Kevin Romanin. Stlying, hair, and makeup by Kim Persley. Production assistance and set photography by Andrew Armstrong. Funded by Telus and Public Records.

title: Boy
description: A label was interested in me, I was set up in a shared studio with Chris, and finally felt really capable of crafting sounds I liked. But the label applied pressure to release even though I wasn’t totally happy with what I’d written, and they didn’t actually do anything to support me. I was mellowing out my sound, wanting to sound more natural in my tones and to let everything breathe. Around this time I also felt more people wanting to see what I’d wear to a show than caring about the songs I was writing. It felt like the party was over, so I wrapped it up.
credits: Everything by Aaron Bergunder. Released on Hybridity. Many thanks to Chris Long as a handful of the songs were reproductions of our Buddies, and I used his Juno in our shared studio space as much as I possibly could.

title: Freak Heat Waves - Object
description: A quick and dirty live capture of Freak Heat Waves playing in the back of the Fifty-Fifty Art Gallery (at the time nicknamed the Rat Shack).
credits: Filmed and edited by Aaron Bergunder and Kyle Surovy. Freak Heat Waves at this time was Steven Lind, Thomas DiNinno, and James Twiddy. 'Object' isn't on their records, so you had to be there or watch this I guess.

title: Buddies
description: The definitive Dreamboat. My fully out-of-love songs. It had been a multi-year bender of weird relationships. Every time I sat at my computer with a microphone it felt like my subconscious dumped out more cathartic reflections. Confidence in my sexuality and saying “yes” to everything had given me plenty to write about. And I finally got the nerve to ask someone to help me push my sound further. Chris rescued me from my dying Compaq with its pirated Ableton and brought the songs to life in new ways far beyond what I'd known.
credits: Written and produced by Aaron Bergunder. Co-produced, mixed, and mastered by Chris Long. First edition album artwork designed and silkscreened by Scott Bell using a photo by Jason Trill. Second edition artwork designed by Aaron Bergunder with imagery from Colt Studios.

title: My Friend Wallis - Hiding
description: Crystal had this catchy, beautiful, summery tune and Kyle and I had cameras. We caught a perfect Victoria afternoon with friends and made it a bit more trippy to drift around with the reverberations.
credits: Filmed and edited by Aaron Bergunder and Kyle Surovy. My Friend Wallis was the musical project of Crystal Dorval.

title: Moon Doom
description: Seven songs forming a narrative of going to the moon and dying and being at peace. These were busy times of partying, I was way over my head in debt, and depressed but unwilling to admit it. In hindsight, fantasizing of jettisoning to space and ending it all makes a lot of sense for what I’d write about. But at the time I just thought I was doing a Bowie thing. This should have been a different band and I regret that. Kyle Bee and Drew McIlmoyl joined (nothing against them, they were always eager and fun), and I was a jerk to Talia and pushed her out using her other band’s schedule as an excuse. Doomed it. Also didn't want to burn CDs again (only vinyl mattered and we couldn't afford that) so I tried to mail out postcards from the moon with album codes.
credits: Songs by Kyle Bee, Aaron Bergunder, Drew McIlmoyl, and Talia Este. Recorded and mixed by Aaron Bergunder. Photography and cover art by Scott Bell.

title: What's Happenin'
description: We worked on these songs a long time, writing and performing parts of them starting on the 2008 (No condoms!) tour. I think we knew Brock was leaving and we needed to get some record of these songs, but the end of the band was something we couldn’t really face. We never properly finished or mastered or released it and I just uploaded it to Bandcamp years later so it wouldn’t be lost.
credits: All songs by Aaron Bergunder, Brock Edwards, Jordan Minkoff, Luke Postl, and James Twiddy. Recorded and mixed by Danny at that big house on the ocean. Photo by Tara in some small nook of Jeff the House.

title: Fun Fools EP
description: With these songs I started to find the sound I felt like I’d been chasing for years. With the perspective gained from a couple years of performing, I could write with an audience in mind. Life was feeling fresh and full of new experiences. Less heartbroken autobiography, this was about manifesting more joyous nights at Lucky Bar.

title: No Coal Christmas EP
description: Hey, why not try to write a whole EP for one show? I think it was Blake Enemark putting on the Christmas church show, and everyone was doing classics and covers. But I'm bad at learning songs so Talia and I just wrote a handful of new ones instead. The show was not our strongest performance, and we almost got stranded in the snow that night. But I still listen to these each Christmas.
credits: Songs by Aaron Bergunder and Talia Este. Recorded and mixed by Aaron Bergunder. Artwork by Michael Noppers.

title: Radcity (7-Eleven) EP
description: Andy “Balacade” Reynolds wanted to tour, so I told him I’d write some songs and be his opener. I asked Talia Este (who had been making great songs herself on Old Life Records) to join me, and in about a month we had this handful of tracks around a concept of nostalgia for when we were eight-year-olds. It was the 90s kids trend before it was a trend, I guess. Everything came together so fast that there was no second-guessing. I think I have the most fondness for these songs because of that.
credits: Songs by Aaron Bergunder and Talia Este. Recorded and mixed by Aaron Bergunder. Artwork by Michael Noppers.

title: Colourbook (Krakatoa)
description: Our young hearts poured out, influences worn proudly, as energetic and chaotic as our lives felt. Each time we wrote one of these songs was like a new discovery of what we could do. It's the sound of Jeff the House. The Wimbledon Riot always felt like my biggest contribution to the band; I wrote it about being in the closet, doing my best to obscure the meaning so I could cathartically sing it and not fully out myself. Jordan and I rarely told each other what our songs were about. It was fun to just match the feeling and layer on whatever we wanted. It was also tough to be genuinely trying very hard to make the best record we could and still have people call us sloppy and showing potential. We'd never admit how good we wanted to be.
credits: All songs by Aaron Bergunder, Brock Edwards, Jordan Minkoff, Luke Postl, and James Twiddy. Recorded and mixed by Matt Skillings and Thomas Shields (Run Chico Run) with a lot of dubs and post by all of us. Self-released in 2007 with artwork by Caleb Beyers (shown). Re-mastered and re-released on Aaaargh! in 2008 with artwork by Caitlin Gallupe.

title: Dreamboat
description: I have very few memories of creating these songs. Some were from my earlier project Karaoke MF and they spilled into this. I was pirating a lot of software and messing around. I clearly wanted to let out all the pent up turmoil from being in the closet. Peaches was a big inspiration. I no longer have a copy of this CD and can't find the artwork file, so this is an old screenshot of Ableton I'd taken around that time and is probably relevant.
credits: Everything by Aaron Bergunder. Much encouragement provided by many friends.
